branch.title: Git branch name, if not filled in, the default is the main branch

commit.title: Commits that require checkout will not be checked out if they are not filled in.

depth.title: The depth of the clone. Do not fill in the entire history of the clone. Fill in 1 to clone only the latest commit. This can reduce the cloning time and cannot be used at the same time as commit.

endpoint.title: S3 access endpoint, which can be a domain name or IP

DatasetStatusPhase.title: - PENDING: The status that has just been created - READY: The status that can be mounted - PROCESSING: The status that is being processed - FAILED: The status that failed to be processed and cannot be mounted

accessPath.title: The access path of the model, such as /v2/llama/generate, needs to be spliced ​​with .status.access_base_url if you want to be able to access it directly.

priorityClass.title: When action = CHANGE_PRIORITY, priority_class needs to be passed in

default.title: If default = true, it indicates the scheduler that should be selected by default. This may be the configuration configured in training-center.

scope.description: Whether the registry is integrated in platform or workspace.

visible.description: Whether the project is public or private.

KubeVolumeVolumeType.description:
- PERSISTENT_VOLUME_CLAIM: The volume is a persistent volume claim.
 - MODEL: The volume is a model.

ModelInputFormat.description:
- FORMAT_NONE: @@    .. cpp:enumerator:: Format::FORMAT_NONE = 0
@@
@@       The input has no specific format. This is the default.
@@
 - FORMAT_NHWC: @@    .. cpp:enumerator:: Format::FORMAT_NHWC = 1
@@
@@       HWC image format. Tensors with this format require 3 dimensions
@@       if the model does not support batching (max_batch_size = 0) or 4
@@       dimensions if the model does support batching (max_batch_size
@@       >= 1). In either case the 'dims' below should only specify the
@@       3 non-batch dimensions (i.e. HWC or CHW).
@@
 - FORMAT_NCHW: @@    .. cpp:enumerator:: Format::FORMAT_NCHW = 2
@@
@@       CHW image format. Tensors with this format require 3 dimensions
@@       if the model does not support batching (max_batch_size = 0) or 4
@@       dimensions if the model does support batching (max_batch_size
@@       >= 1). In either case the 'dims' below should only specify the
@@       3 non-batch dimensions (i.e. HWC or CHW).
@@
ModelInputFormat.title:
@@
@@  .. cpp:enum:: Format
@@
@@     The format for the input.
@@

NotebookConfigServiceType.description:
- NODE_PORT: The notebook is exposed as a NodePort service.
 - LOAD_BALANCER: The notebook is exposed as a LoadBalancer service.

host.title: SSH domain name/IP, if empty, use browser configuration.

port.title: Port for SSH, if 0, use browser configuration.

SecretOptionsAuthType.title: - AUTH_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: No authentication required - BASIC: Use username and password authentication - SSH: Use SSH certificate authentication - TOKEN: Use Token authentication - AK_SK: Use AK/SK authentication

customModelConfig.title:
Can be used to customize the model configuration of the python backend, such as model.py: "import torch
import torchvision
from torchvision import models

model = models.resnet50(pretrained=True)
model.eval()" Not used for the time being

StatusPhase.description:
- PENDING: The analysis is being prepared for use.
 - RUNNING: The analysis is active and ready to use.
 - STOPPED: The analysis is being stopped.
 - FAILED: The analysis is failed.

TritonBackend.title: - TRITON_BACKEND_VLLM: deprecated: DO NOT USE ME

relativePath.title: The location of the model file relative to the dataset

volume.title: For data sets, there is no need to pass mountPath, it will be done automatically.

commonAffinity.description:
The following message is from k8s official: https://github.com/kubernetes/api/blob/master/core/v1/generated.proto
Affinity is a group of affinity scheduling rules.

nodeAffinity.description: Describes node affinity scheduling rules for the pod.

podAffinity.description: Describes pod affinity scheduling rules (e.g. co-locate this pod in the same node, zone, etc. as some other pod(s)).

podAntiAffinity.description: Describes pod anti-affinity scheduling rules (e.g. avoid putting this pod in the same node, zone, etc. as some other pod(s)).

dataDirection.title: Data flow type, input or output

name.description:
if the volume is a model, the name should be the format of "model_name/version".
if you set the name to "model_name", the version will be set to the latest version.

matchExpressions.description: matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The requirements are ANDed.

matchLabels.description:
matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels
map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is "key", the
operator is "In", and the values array contains only "value". The requirements are ANDed.

key.title:
key is the label key that the selector applies to.
+patchMergeKey=key
+patchStrategy=merge

operator.description:
operator represents a key's relationship to a set of values.
Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values.description:
values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic
merge patch.

commonNodeAffinity.description: Node affinity is a group of node affinity scheduling rules.

preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringEx.description:
The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy
the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose
a node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is
most preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.
for each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource
request, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),
compute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding
"weight" to the sum if the node matches the corresponding matchExpressions; the
node(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.

requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExe.description:
If the affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at
scheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node.
If the affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met
at some point during pod execution (e.g. due to an update), the system
may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.

commonNodeSelector.title:
A node selector represents the union of the results of one or more label queries
over a set of nodes; that is, it represents the OR of the selectors represented
by the node selector terms.
+structType=atomic

nodeSelectorTerms.description: Required. A list of node selector terms. The terms are ORed.

commonNodeSelectorRequirement.description:
A node selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator
that relates the key and values.

key.description: The label key that the selector applies to.

operator.description:
Represents a key's relationship to a set of values.
Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. Gt, and Lt.

values.description:
An array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. If the operator is Gt or Lt, the values
array must have a single element, which will be interpreted as an integer.
This array is replaced during a strategic merge patch.

commonNodeSelectorTerm.title:
A null or empty node selector term matches no objects. The requirements of
them are ANDed.
The TopologySelectorTerm type implements a subset of the NodeSelectorTerm.
+structType=atomic

matchExpressions.description: A list of node selector requirements by node's labels.

matchFields.description: A list of node selector requirements by node's fields.

page.title: The current page index starts from 1. When it is 0, it will be automatically reset to the default value constants.DefaultPage

pageSize.title: The amount of data per page. When it is -1, it means querying all. When it is 0, it will be reset to the default value constants.DefaultPageSize

search.description: Search keywords, supporting fuzzy search, precise matching and advanced search.

sort.title: Sorting rules, supporting string and numeric type fields for sorting

total.title: How many entries are there in total? You don’t need to pass them when requesting

commonPodAffinity.description: Pod affinity is a group of inter pod affinity scheduling rules.

preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringEx.description:
The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy
the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose
a node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is
most preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.
for each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource
request, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),
compute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding
"weight" to the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the
node(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.

requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExe.description:
If the affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at
scheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node.
If the affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met
at some point during pod execution (e.g. due to a pod label update), the
system may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.
When there are multiple elements, the lists of nodes corresponding to each
podAffinityTerm are intersected, i.e. all terms must be satisfied.

labelSelector.description: A label query over a set of resources, in this case pods.

namespaceSelector.description:
A label query over the set of namespaces that the term applies to.
The term is applied to the union of the namespaces selected by this field
and the ones listed in the namespaces field.
null selector and null or empty namespaces list means "this pod's namespace".
An empty selector ({}) matches all namespaces.
This field is beta-level and is only honored when PodAffinityNamespaceSelector feature is enabled.

namespaces.title:
namespaces specifies a static list of namespace names that the term applies to.
The term is applied to the union of the namespaces listed in this field
and the ones selected by namespaceSelector.
null or empty namespaces list and null namespaceSelector means "this pod's namespace"

topologyKey.description:
This pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with the pods matching
the labelSelector in the specified namespaces, where co-located is defined as running on a node
whose value of the label with key topologyKey matches that of any node on which any of the
selected pods is running.
Empty topologyKey is not allowed.

commonPodAntiAffinity.description: Pod anti affinity is a group of inter pod anti affinity scheduling rules.

preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringEx.description:
The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy
the anti-affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose
a node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is
most preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.
for each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource
request, requiredDuringScheduling anti-affinity expressions, etc.),
compute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding
"weight" to the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the
node(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.

requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExe.description:
If the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at
scheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node.
If the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met
at some point during pod execution (e.g. due to a pod label update), the
system may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.
When there are multiple elements, the lists of nodes corresponding to each
podAffinityTerm are intersected, i.e. all terms must be satisfied.

commonPodConfig.title:
PodTemplateConfig is a configuration for a pod template,
it includes the environment variables, volumes, resources, affinity and schedulerName.
The configuration is used to create a pod template for serving or job?

kubeEnvs.description: The environment variables of the pod.

priorityClass.title: the priority class of pod. baize-high-priority baize-medium-priority baize-low-priority currently only supports these three, and other priorities of the system may be supported in the future.

schedulerName.description: The name of the scheduler to use.

commonPreferredSchedulingTerm.description:
An empty preferred scheduling term matches all objects with implicit weight 0
(i.e. it's a no-op). A null preferred scheduling term matches no objects (i.e. is also a no-op).

preference.description: A node selector term, associated with the corresponding weight.

weight.description: Weight associated with matching the corresponding nodeSelectorTerm, in the range 1-100.

value.title:
The name of the resource affinity.
For HAMI_VGPU, the name is the GPU card name, eg: NVIDIA-NVIDIA A800 80GB PCIe

commonWeightedPodAffinityTerm.title: The weights of all of the matched WeightedPodAffinityTerm fields are added per-node to find the most preferred node(s)

podAffinityTerm.description: Required. A pod affinity term, associated with the corresponding weight.

weight.description:
weight associated with matching the corresponding podAffinityTerm,
in the range 1-100.

id.description: Error ID, used to determine error type

message.description: Error messages will automatically switch between Chinese and English

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

 The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

id.description: Error ID, used to determine error type

message.description: Error messages will automatically switch between Chinese and English

tritonDataType.description:
- TYPE_INVALID: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::INVALID = 0
 - TYPE_BOOL: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::BOOL = 1
 - TYPE_UINT8: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::UINT8 = 2
 - TYPE_UINT16: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::UINT16 = 3
 - TYPE_UINT32: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::UINT32 = 4
 - TYPE_UINT64: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::UINT64 = 5
 - TYPE_INT8: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::INT8 = 6
 - TYPE_INT16: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::INT16 = 7
 - TYPE_INT32: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::INT32 = 8
 - TYPE_INT64: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::INT64 = 9
 - TYPE_FP16: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::FP16 = 10
 - TYPE_FP32: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::FP32 = 11
 - TYPE_FP64: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::FP64 = 12
 - TYPE_STRING: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::STRING = 13
 - TYPE_BF16: @@  .. cpp:enumerator:: DataType::BF16 = 14
tritonDataType.title:
@@
@@.. cpp:enum:: DataType
@@
@@   Data types supported for input and output tensors.
@@

tritonModelInput.title:
@@
@@.. cpp:var:: message ModelInput
@@
@@   An input required by the model.
@@

allowRaggedBatch.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: bool allow_ragged_batch
@@
@@     Whether or not the input is allowed to be "ragged" in a dynamically
@@     created batch. Default is false indicating that two requests will
@@     only be batched if this tensor has the same shape in both requests.
@@     True indicates that two requests can be batched even if this tensor
@@     has a different shape in each request.
@@

dataType.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: DataType data_type
@@
@@     The data-type of the input.
@@

dims.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: int64 dims (repeated)
@@
@@     The dimensions/shape of the input tensor that must be provided
@@     when invoking the inference API for this model.
@@

format.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: Format format
@@
@@     The format of the input. Optional.
@@

isShapeTensor.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: bool is_shape_tensor
@@
@@     Whether or not the input is a shape tensor to the model. This field
@@     is currently supported only for the TensorRT model. An error will be
@@     generated if this specification does not comply with underlying
@@     model.
@@

name.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: string name
@@
@@     The name of the input.
@@

optional.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: bool optional
@@
@@     Whether or not the input is optional for the model execution.
@@     If true, the input is not required in the inference request.
@@     Default value is false.
@@

reshape.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: ModelTensorReshape reshape
@@
@@     The shape expected for this input by the backend. The input will
@@     be reshaped to this before being presented to the backend. The
@@     reshape must have the same number of elements as the input shape
@@     specified by 'dims'. Optional.
@@

tritonModelOutput.title:
@@
@@.. cpp:var:: message ModelOutput
@@
@@   An output produced by the model.
@@

dataType.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: DataType data_type
@@
@@     The data-type of the output.
@@

dims.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: int64 dims (repeated)
@@
@@     The dimensions/shape of the output tensor.
@@

isShapeTensor.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: bool is_shape_tensor
@@
@@     Whether or not the output is a shape tensor to the model. This field
@@     is currently supported only for the TensorRT model. An error will be
@@     generated if this specification does not comply with underlying
@@     model.
@@

labelFilename.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: string label_filename
@@
@@     The label file associated with this output. Should be specified only
@@     for outputs that represent classifications. Optional.
@@

name.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: string name
@@
@@     The name of the output.
@@

reshape.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: ModelTensorReshape reshape
@@
@@     The shape produced for this output by the backend. The output will
@@     be reshaped from this to the shape specified in 'dims' before being
@@     returned in the inference response. The reshape must have the same
@@     number of elements as the output shape specified by 'dims'. Optional.
@@

tritonModelTensorReshape.title:
@@
@@.. cpp:var:: message ModelTensorReshape
@@
@@   Reshape specification for input and output tensors.
@@

shape.title:
@@  .. cpp:var:: int64 shape (repeated)
@@
@@     The shape to use for reshaping.
@@

kubeVolumes.description: Which volume to mount to the monitor.

logPath.description: The path that contains the tensorboard, visualdl, or other monitor logs.

kubeVolumes.description: Which volume to mount to the monitor.

logPath.description: The path that contains the tensorboard, visualdl, or other monitor logs.

accessUrl.description: The url of the analysis (tensorboard, visualdl) panel.

v1alpha1AnalysisType.description:
Different types of monitor.

 - TENSORBOARD: The monitor as TensorBoard.

v1alpha1Artifact.description:
Artifact is corresponding to a digest in registry. For example
the `706446e9` part in `dockhub.io/library/nginx@sha256:706446e9` .
Note a digest could have multiple tags, such as: `latest`, `v0.1`.

imageSize.description: Size of artifact. Unit: byte. 1024 GenericBinary.

tags.description: Tags is a list of tags.

uri.title: Data source address. Data sets other than GIT types need to start with a protocol, such as: http://, https://, s3://, pvc://, nfs://, dataset://, huggingface://, modelscope://

gpuType.title: Whether to use the GPU, empty means not to use the GPU and directly pass the Resources name of the GPU card, such as nvidia.com/gpu, or nvidia.com/vgpu

endpoint.title: The endpoint address of HuggingFace can be a domain name or IP, and needs to start with a protocol, such as: https://, http://

exclude.title: When pulling files, exclude files matching glob

include.title: When pulling files, which glob matching files are included?

repoType.title: It is the repo type of HuggingFace and currently supports model

revision.title: Pulled revision version/commit, default fallback to the latest version judged by huggingface-cli

exclude.title: When pulling files, exclude files matching glob

include.title: When pulling files, which glob matching files are included?

repoType.title: It is the repo type of HuggingFace and currently supports model

revision.title: Pull the revision version/commit, the default fallback is the latest version judged by the modelscope CLI

pvcName.title: The name of the PVC to be mounted

secretRef.title: Secret name of the data source's sensitive data store.

storageClassName.title:
currently we do not support custom pvc name.
 string name = 1;

status.title: Dataset status, only needs to be displayed when phase is PROCESSING

syncProcess.title: Warm-up progress, a value between 0 - 100, expressed as a percentage.

objKind.description: The kind of the object that this event is about.

objName.description: The name of the object that this event is about.

objNamespace.description: The namespace of the object that this event is about.

type.title: Inference framework type, currently supports Triton

aliasZh.title: gpu workload type resources zh alias

v1alpha1GPUSettingsResponse.title: GPUSettingResponse is the response message for GPUSetting

permission.title: Operation and maintenance management console permissions

hpaConfig.title: list serving does not return hpaConfig

models.title: Model status currently only supports one. The array is used here just for expansion.

totalResources.title: Total task resources (statistics for all containers)

imageConfig.description: The image info of the notebook. if not empty, means the image is selected from kangaroo.

shmSize.title: The size of the SHM space in MB. 0 means no SHM space is allocated.

v1alpha1JobPhase.title: - JOB_PHASE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified type. - CREATED: The task was created successfully. - RUNNING: The task is running. - FAILED: The task failed to run. - SUCCEEDED: The task ran successfully. - SUSPENDED: The task is stopped.

v1alpha1ListArtifactsResponse.title: ListArtifactsResponse returns a list of tags of specified image

items.description: Items is a list of tag names.

page.description: Pagination returned contains current page, size, and total.

v1alpha1ListProjectsResponse.title: ListProjectsResponse returns a list of projects of a registry

items.description: Items is a list of project names.

page.description: Pagination returned contains current page, size, and total.

v1alpha1ListRegistriesResponse.title: ListRegistriesResponse returns a list of registries

page.description: Pagination returned contains current page, size, and total.

v1alpha1ListRepositoriesResponse.title: ListRepositoriesResponse returns a list of projects of a registry

items.description: Items is a list of repositories.

page.description: Pagination returned contains current page, size, and total.

name.title: If it is nvidia gpu, the name is: kpanda_gpu_pod_utilization, see: https://gitlab.daocloud.cn/ndx/engineering/kpanda/-/blob/master/docs/design/metrics/gpu-metrics.md cpu is: cpu memory is: memory

sharedToWorkspaces.title: The target Workspace that has access to this model

snapshotConfig.title:
SnapshotConfig describes the config of snapshot for running notebook
More info refer: https://github.com/BaizeAI/kube-snapshot/

enableSSH.title: Control whether ssh service is enabled on notebooks

imageConfig.description: The image info of the notebook. if not empty, means the image is selected from kangaroo.

message.description:
A human readable message indicating details about why the notebook is
in this condition.

v1alpha1NotebookStatusPhase.description:
- PENDING: The notebook is being prepared for use.
 - RUNNING: The notebook is active and ready to use.
 - STOPPED: The notebook is being stopped.
 - FAILED: The notebook is failed.

resourceType.title: Resource type, may be * * for all resources.

v1alpha1Project.title:
Project is the second segment of path of the image, for example
the `library` part for `dockhub.io/library/nginx@sha256:706446e9`

name.description: Name is registry name, use to query project lists.

v1alpha1Repository.description:
Repository is the name of a image, for example,
the `nginx` part for `dockhub.io/library/nginx@sha256:706446e9`
Note the repository name can have multiple segments in path.
for example: for the image `release-ci.daocloud.io/docker.io/library/a/b/c:latest`
the repository name will be `library/a/b/c`.

v1alpha1RestartPolicy.title: - RESTART_POLICY_NEVER: If the task fails, do not restart the task. - RESTART_POLICY_ON_FAILURE: If the task fails, restart the task,

autoSaveOnTermination.title: auto_save_on_termination will automatic build and save pod's image before termination

imageConfig.title:
registry address and project name, for now we only support
kangaroo registry and project

v1alpha1TrainingMode.title: - TRAINING_MODE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified type. - SINGLE: Standalone mode. - DISTRIBUTED: Distributed mode.

isBaizeOwner.title: string name =1; Is it a Baize platform administrator?

creationTimestamp.title: No settings are required when creating
